meta.siteDescription: We build turnkey websites for experts, businesses and e-commerce that help attract leads, sell services and convert traffic into clients.
meta.ogTitle: NAKO Agency | Turnkey Websites for Business & Experts
meta.ogDescription: Strategy, design and development in one solution. Websites for experts, businesses, e-commerce, courses and events.
meta.keywords: website development, web design, web studio, turnkey website, landing page, online store, expert website, business website, app development

items.title: RESULTS
items.subtitle: Websites for business results
items.description: We build websites not for beauty, but for leads, sales and trust. Every element works towards conversion and commercial results.

items.title: FULL CYCLE
items.subtitle: From brief to support
items.description: Strategy, structure, copy, design, development, content, integrations, launch and support — all in one place. No need to find separate contractors.

items.title: PRESENTATION
items.subtitle: Premium design + sales logic
items.description: We combine stylish visual presentation with a well-thought-out structure and conversion logic. Premium, calm, modern — without unnecessary noise.

items.title: INTEGRATIONS
items.subtitle: We integrate your website into business processes
items.description: CRM, Telegram, email, analytics, payments — we connect everything needed so your website works as part of your system, not just a business card.

items.title: SUPPORT
items.subtitle: We stay after launch
items.description: We don't abandon you after delivery. Bugfixes, minor edits, consultations, monthly support — choose a convenient support format.

items.title: Expert websites
items.description: Websites for experts, consultants, mentors and specialists who need a strong personal brand and a flow of leads.

items.title: Business websites
items.description: Websites for companies, studios and local businesses to present services, build trust and attract clients.

items.title: Landing pages
items.description: Landing pages for services, products, advertising, launches and special offers.

items.title: Online stores
items.description: User-friendly online stores with well-thought-out structure, catalog, product cards and purchase logic.

items.title: Course websites
items.description: Websites for educational projects, online courses and schools with clear structure and strong presentation.

items.title: Event websites
items.description: Websites for events, conferences, presentations, workshops and special projects.

steps.title: Strategy & Brief
steps.description: We analyze your goals, audience, and positioning. Fill out the brief, define website objectives and key result metrics.

steps.title: Structure & Prototype
steps.description: We design site architecture, user scenarios, and wireframes. Align on logic and navigation before moving to design.

steps.title: Design
steps.description: We create an individual visual style. Every screen is a thoughtful composition with attention to typography and detail.

steps.title: Development & Integrations
steps.description: We build on Next.js with focus on speed and SEO. Connect CRM, Telegram, email, analytics, and payments.

steps.title: Content & Copy
steps.description: We fill the site with content: text, photos, media. Help with messaging, copywriting, and visual selection.

steps.title: Testing
steps.description: We test on all devices and browsers. Check forms, integrations, user scenarios, and performance.

steps.title: Launch
steps.description: We deploy to production, set up domain, SSL, and analytics. Hand over access, documentation, and train you on site management.

steps.title: Support
steps.description: We stay in touch after launch. Bug fixes, updates, minor edits, and project development within a support package.

items.name: Anna Kovaleva
items.quote: The website paid for itself in the first month. Bookings tripled, and clients come already 'warm' — they've read everything about me on the site.

items.name: Maxim Petrov
items.quote: Finally the store works fast. Before, clients left because of slow loading. Now conversion has grown by 40%.

items.name: Elena Sokolova
items.quote: Really liked the approach — not just a pretty picture, but a thought-out structure. Every block works for conversion.

items.name: Dmitry Volkov
items.quote: Launched the landing in 2 weeks. Quality on par with top agencies, but without their budgets and bureaucracy.

items.name: Olga Mironova
items.quote: The website captures the studio atmosphere perfectly. Minimalism, space, calm — exactly what was needed.

faq.caption: Answers
faq.title: FAQ
faq.ctaSubtitle: Contact us — we'll reply within an hour

items.question: How long does development take?
items.answer: Timelines depend on project complexity. Landing page — up to 14 days, expert website — up to 21 days, online store — up to 45 days. Exact timelines are discussed after filling out the brief.

items.question: How much does a website cost? What affects the price?
items.answer: Cost depends on website type, number of pages, design complexity, and integrations. Exact cost is calculated individually after filling out the brief.

items.question: What's included in turnkey development?
items.answer: Full cycle: brief and analytics, prototyping, unique design, responsive coding, basic SEO, analytics setup, testing and launch. You get a website ready to work.

items.question: Do I need a domain and hosting?
items.answer: Domain needs to be registered in advance or we'll help with selection and registration. Hosting — we use modern platforms (Vercel, Netlify) with free tiers for most projects.

items.question: How many revisions are included?
items.answer: Each stage includes a round of revisions: prototype, design, coding. Minor corrections — unlimited. Major concept changes are quoted separately.

items.question: Is there support after launch?
items.answer: Yes, 30 days of free support after launch: bug fixes, minor edits, consultations. After that — by support agreement or on-demand.

items.question: Will the website be SEO-optimized?
items.answer: Basic SEO is included: semantic markup, meta tags, sitemap, robots.txt, image optimization, loading speed. Promotion and content marketing — separate service.

items.question: What integrations are possible?
items.answer: Any: CRM (amoCRM, Bitrix24), payment systems (Stripe, PayPal), email newsletters, Telegram bots, booking calendars, analytics (GA4, etc.) and others on request.

items.question: How does payment work?
items.answer: In stages: 50% prepayment before starting, 50% after completion and acceptance. For large projects, splitting into 3 stages is possible. We work by contract.

items.question: What if I don't like the design?
items.answer: We approve each stage before moving to the next. If the design doesn't suit after the first iteration — we offer 2 alternative concepts. In case of complete disagreement — refund per contract.

ru152fz.title: Consent to Personal Data Processing
sections: I, the data subject, hereby freely, voluntarily and in my own interest give my consent to NAKO Agency (hereinafter — the Operator) for the processing of my personal data in accordance with Federal Law No. 152-FZ of 27.07.2006 'On Personal Data'.

The following personal data is subject to processing: surname, first name, email address, telephone number, messenger username, and other data voluntarily provided by me when filling out forms on the website.
Purposes of personal data processing: processing incoming requests; contacting the data subject to discuss a project; sending informational and commercial messages (with separate consent).

Processing methods: collection, recording, systematisation, accumulation, storage, clarification, extraction, use, transfer (provision, access), anonymisation, blocking, deletion, destruction of personal data — with and without the use of automation tools.
Validity period: from the moment of provision until withdrawal. Consent may be withdrawn by sending a written notification to hello@nakoagency.com.
The Operator undertakes to ensure the security of personal data and take necessary measures to prevent unauthorised access.

about.metaDescription: A web studio focused on results. We build websites for experts, e-commerce and business. Learn about our approach, values and work principles.
about.heroDescription: We are an agency that builds modern turnkey websites for experts, businesses and digital projects, combining strategy, design and development in one solution. Our goal is to make websites not just visually beautiful, but commercially strong and working for leads.
about.approachText1: We build websites not for the sake of having one, but for business results. Every project starts with understanding your goals and audience.
about.approachText2: Full cycle: from brief and messaging to launch and support. Strategy → Structure → Design → Development → Content → Launch.
about.approachText3: Premium visual presentation with sales and conversion logic. We remove the unnecessary to leave what converts visitors into clients.

values.title: Results
values.description: A website is a tool. Beauty without conversion is meaningless. We create what works.

values.title: Transparency
values.description: Fixed timelines, clear stages, regular reports. You always know where the project stands.

values.title: Quality
values.description: Clean code, fast loading, SEO out of the box. We do it right so it doesn't need redoing.

values.title: Partnership
values.description: Not contractors, but partners. We advise, suggest, and debate — if it's in the project's interest.

principles.title: Strategy first
principles.description: We don't start design until we understand goals and audience.

principles.title: Less is more
principles.description: Every element must work. Decoration for decoration's sake is not our way.

principles.title: Mobile-first
principles.description: 60%+ traffic is mobile. Phone first, then desktop.

principles.title: Speed is critical
principles.description: Lighthouse 90+ is the standard. Fast site = more conversions.

principles.title: SEO from day one
principles.description: Not 'we'll fix it later', but right away: semantics, meta tags, structure.

principles.title: Support included
principles.description: 30 days after launch. Bugs, edits, questions — we handle it.

privacy.metaDescription: NAKO Agency privacy policy. Information about collection, use and protection of personal data.
privacy.caption: Legal information

sections.title: General Provisions
content: NAKO Agency ("we", "our", "Company") acts as the data controller for personal data collected through this website.
Website: nakoagency.com.
This Privacy Policy applies to all users worldwide, regardless of location.
We operate across the following jurisdictions: Moldova, Romania, Ukraine, Poland, Germany, Italy, Russia, and Kazakhstan.

Applicable data protection regulations include: the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) for the European Union; Law No. 195/2024 on Personal Data Protection (Moldova); Law No. 2297-VI on Personal Data Protection (Ukraine); Federal Law No. 152-FZ on Personal Data (Russia); Law No. 94-V on Personal Data and Their Protection (Kazakhstan).

sections.title: Data We Collect
content: Personal data provided through forms: name, email address, phone number, Telegram username, company name, and project description.
Automatically collected data: IP address (anonymized for EU users), browser type, device information, pages visited, and referrer URL.
Cookies: we use necessary, analytics, marketing, and preference cookies. For details, please see our Cookie Policy.
Third-party services that may collect data: Google Analytics 4 and Vercel Analytics.

sections.title: Purposes and Legal Basis
content: Processing form submissions — legal basis: your consent (Art. 6(1)(a) GDPR).
Communication about projects and services — legal basis: contractual necessity (Art. 6(1)(b) GDPR).
Website improvement and analytics — legal basis: legitimate interest (Art. 6(1)(f) GDPR).
Legal compliance — legal basis: legal obligation (Art. 6(1)(c) GDPR).
Marketing communications (only with your explicit consent) — legal basis: consent (Art. 6(1)(a) GDPR).

sections.title: Cookies and Tracking
content: We use the following categories of cookies: necessary (always active), analytics (opt-in), marketing (opt-in), and preferences (opt-in).
Google Consent Mode v2 is implemented on our website.
Before you provide consent, no non-essential cookies are set.
You can manage your cookie preferences via the cookie banner displayed on your first visit, or through the "Cookie Settings" link in the website footer at any time.
For full details on the cookies we use, their purposes, and retention periods, please see our Cookie Policy page.

sections.title: Data Sharing
content: We do NOT sell your personal data under any circumstances.

We share data with the following service providers (data processors): Vercel Inc. (hosting, United States — protected by EU Standard Contractual Clauses); Resend Inc. (email delivery, United States — protected by EU Standard Contractual Clauses); Google LLC (analytics, United States — protected by EU Standard Contractual Clauses).
We may disclose data to legal authorities upon lawful request.
All third-party processors are bound by Data Processing Agreements (DPAs) that ensure an adequate level of data protection.

sections.title: International Data Transfers
content: Our website is hosted on the Vercel Inc. platform (USA). When you submit a form, your data is processed in transit by Vercel servers located in the USA and EU, and immediately forwarded to our email and Telegram. Personal data is not stored on Vercel servers or in any databases.
By submitting a form, you consent to the cross-border transfer of your data to the specified jurisdictions.
Third-party services and their locations: Vercel Inc. (USA/EU) — hosting and transit form processing; Google LLC (USA) — analytics (Google Analytics 4); Resend Inc. (USA) — email delivery.
Safeguards for EU residents: data transfers to the USA are based on EU Standard Contractual Clauses (SCCs) concluded between us and our service providers. All data is encrypted in transit (TLS 1.2+).

For Russian residents (152-FZ): personal data submitted through website forms is processed in transit and is not stored on servers outside the Russian Federation. Data is forwarded to the operator's email and messenger. By submitting a form, you confirm your consent to cross-border data transfer in accordance with Art. 12 of Federal Law No. 152-FZ.

For Kazakh residents (Law No. 94-V): personal data is processed in transit and is not stored on servers. By submitting a form, you confirm your consent to cross-border data transfer in accordance with the legislation of the Republic of Kazakhstan on personal data.
For residents of Moldova, Ukraine, Poland, Germany, Italy, and Romania: safeguards provided by the GDPR (EU Regulation 2016/679) and the respective national legislation apply.

sections.title: Data Retention
content: Form submissions: retained for 2 years from the date of submission, then permanently deleted.
Analytics data: retained for 14 months (Google Analytics 4 standard retention period).
Cookies: retention varies by type; see our Cookie Policy for specific durations.
Cookie consent records: retained for 3 years as proof of GDPR compliance.
Upon your request: personal data will be deleted within 30 days.

sections.title: Your Rights
content: All users have the right to: access their personal data, request rectification of inaccurate data, request deletion of their data, and withdraw consent to data processing.

EU residents (under GDPR) additionally have the right to: data portability, restriction of processing, objection to processing, and the right not to be subject to automated decision-making. Relevant supervisory authorities: ANSPDCP (Romania), UODO (Poland), BfDI (Germany), Garante per la protezione dei dati personali (Italy).
Russian residents (under 152-FZ) have the right to: access, correction, blocking, and destruction of personal data, as well as withdrawal of consent. Supervisory authority: Roskomnadzor.
Kazakh residents (under Law No. 94-V) have the right to: access, correction, and deletion of personal data. Contact: Committee on Information Security of the Ministry of Digital Development.
Moldovan residents (under Law No. 195/2024) will have GDPR-equivalent rights from August 2026. Supervisory authority: National Centre for Personal Data Protection (NCPDP).

Ukrainian residents (under Law No. 2297-VI) have the right to: access, correction, deletion of personal data, and withdrawal of consent. Supervisory authority: Ukrainian Parliament Commissioner for Human Rights (Ombudsman of Ukraine).
To exercise any of your rights, please contact us at: hello@nakoagency.com. We will respond within 30 days of receiving your request.

sections.title: Data Security
content: All data is encrypted in transit using HTTPS/TLS protocols.
Access to personal data is restricted to authorized personnel only.
We conduct regular security reviews of our systems and processes.
While no method of electronic transmission or storage is 100% secure, in the event of a data breach we will notify affected users within 72 hours as required by the GDPR.

sections.title: Children's Privacy
content: This website is not intended for use by children.
Age thresholds for consent vary by jurisdiction: under 16 in Germany and the Netherlands; under 14 in Italy; under 13 in Poland and by default in other jurisdictions.
We do not knowingly collect personal data from children. If you believe a child has provided us with personal data, please contact us immediately.

sections.title: Changes to This Policy
content: We may update this Privacy Policy from time to time. Changes become effective upon publication on the website.
In the event of material changes, users will be notified via a banner on the website.
The date of the last update is indicated at the top of this document.

sections.title: Contact Information
content: Data Controller: NAKO Agency.
Email: hello@nakoagency.com.
Website: nakoagency.com.
For GDPR-specific inquiries, please write to: hello@nakoagency.com.

sections.title: General Provisions
content: These Terms of Use (hereinafter — "Terms") govern the use of the website nakoagency.com (hereinafter — "Website") operated by NAKO Agency (hereinafter — "Company", "we", "our").
By accessing and using the Website, you agree to these Terms. If you do not agree, please stop using the Website.

content: The Company provides turnkey website development services, including:
Website strategy and planning.
UI/UX design.

Detailed description of services and conditions is provided during individual consultation.

sections.title: Website Usage

Not use the Website for illegal purposes.
Not attempt to gain unauthorized access to the Website or its systems.

sections.title: Intellectual Property

Design, layout, and visual elements.
Texts, images, and graphics.
Source code and software solutions.
Logos and trademarks.

sections.title: Service Delivery Procedure
content: Services are provided on the basis of:
Application through the Website form or email.
Discussion of requirements and approval of the brief.
Signing of the service agreement.
Stage-by-stage delivery and approval.
Specific conditions, timelines, and costs are determined individually for each project.

sections.title: Payment for Services
content: Payment terms:
Payment is made according to the terms specified in the agreement.
Standard payment scheme: 50% prepayment, 50% upon completion.
For large projects, payment may be split into 3 stages.
Payment methods are agreed upon individually.
Refund terms are specified in the agreement.

sections.title: Limitation of Liability
content: The Company shall not be liable for:
Temporary unavailability of the Website due to technical maintenance.
Losses arising from the use or inability to use the Website.
Actions of third parties, including hosting providers and payment systems.
Force majeure circumstances.
The Company's total liability is limited to the amount paid by the client under the agreement.

sections.title: Disclaimer of Warranties
content: The Website is provided "as is". The Company does not guarantee:
Uninterrupted Website operation.
Absence of technical errors.
Compliance of the Website with your specific requirements.
Accuracy and completeness of information posted on the Website.
We strive to ensure the highest quality but cannot guarantee the absence of any defects.

sections.title: Links to Third-Party Resources
content: The Website may contain links to third-party websites. We are not responsible for:
Content of third-party websites.
Privacy policies of third-party resources.
Any losses arising from the use of third-party resources.

sections.title: Changes to Terms
content: We reserve the right to modify these Terms at any time without prior notice. Changes take effect upon publication on the Website.
Continued use of the Website after changes have been made constitutes your acceptance of the new Terms.
The date of the last update is indicated at the top of the document.

sections.title: Applicable Law and Jurisdiction
content: These Terms are governed by the laws applicable in the user's jurisdiction.
For EU residents (Romania, Poland, Germany, Italy): disputes are subject to GDPR and the courts of the user's EU member state.
For Moldovan residents: governed by the laws of the Republic of Moldova.
For Ukrainian residents: governed by the laws of Ukraine.
For Russian residents: governed by the laws of the Russian Federation. Disputes are resolved in courts of the Russian Federation.
For Kazakh residents: governed by the laws of the Republic of Kazakhstan.
In all other cases: governed by the laws of Romania as the Company's principal place of operation.

sections.title: Age Restrictions
content: By using this Website, you confirm that you meet the minimum age requirement in your jurisdiction:
Germany, Netherlands: 16 years.
Italy, France: 14 years.
Poland, Romania, Moldova, Ukraine: 13 years.
Russia, Kazakhstan: 14 years.
All other countries: 13 years.
If you are under the required age, you must obtain parental consent before using this Website.

sections.title: Contact Information
content: If you have questions regarding these Terms, contact us:
Email: hello@nakoagency.com
Website: nakoagency.com

cookiePolicy.metaDescription: NAKO Agency cookie policy. Detailed information about cookies we use, their purposes and how to manage them.
cookiePolicy.caption: Legal information

cookieTable.name: _ga
cookieTable.purpose: Distinguishes unique users by assigning a randomly generated number as a client identifier

cookieTable.name: _ga_*
cookieTable.purpose: Maintains session state and tracks user interactions across pages

cookieTable.name: NEXT_LOCALE
cookieTable.purpose: Stores the user's preferred language for the website interface

cookieTable.name: user-region
cookieTable.purpose: Stores the detected or selected user region for localized content and pricing

cookieTable.name: cc_status
cookieTable.purpose: Stores the user's cookie consent preferences and choices

cookieTable.name: _vercel_*
cookieTable.purpose: Used for platform analytics, performance monitoring and deployment optimization

sections.title: What Are Cookies
content: Cookies are small text files that are stored on your device when you visit a website. They help the website remember your preferences and improve your browsing experience.
Cookies can be "session" cookies, which are deleted when you close your browser, or "persistent" cookies, which remain on your device for a set period of time.

sections.title: How We Use Cookies
content: We use cookies to ensure the proper functioning of our website, to analyze traffic and user behavior, and to remember your language and region preferences.
Cookies also help us understand which pages are most popular and how visitors navigate the site, allowing us to continuously improve our services.

sections.title: Cookie Categories
content: Necessary cookies are essential for the website to function properly. They enable core features such as language selection, cookie consent storage, and security.
Analytics cookies help us understand how visitors interact with our website by collecting and reporting information anonymously.
Preferences cookies allow the website to remember your choices such as region and display settings to provide a more personalized experience.
Marketing cookies are used to track visitors across websites to display relevant advertisements. We currently do not use marketing cookies.

sections.title: Managing Cookies
content: When you first visit our website, a cookie banner will appear allowing you to accept or reject non-essential cookies. You can change your preferences at any time through the cookie settings link in the footer.
You can also manage cookies through your browser settings. Most browsers allow you to block or delete cookies. Please note that disabling certain cookies may affect website functionality.

sections.title: Third-Party Cookies
content: We use Google Analytics to analyze website traffic. Google may set cookies on your device to collect anonymous usage data. For more information, visit Google's privacy policy.
Our website is hosted on Vercel, which may set technical cookies necessary for content delivery and platform performance.

sections.title: Contact
content: If you have any questions about our use of cookies, please contact us at hello@nakoagency.com. We are happy to provide additional information about our data practices.

impressum.metaDescription: Legal disclosure and company information for NAKO Agency as required by German Telemediengesetz (TMG §5).
impressum.caption: Legal information

why-nextjs-for-business.title: Why Next.js Is the Best Choice for a Business Website in 2024
why-nextjs-for-business.excerpt: We break down the advantages of Next.js for commercial projects: SEO, performance, scalability.
why-nextjs-for-business.author: NAKO Agency Team
why-nextjs-for-business.content:
Introduction

Next.js has become the standard for building modern web applications. In 2024, this is especially relevant for business websites where SEO, loading speed, and scalability matter.

In this article, we'll break down why we choose Next.js for commercial projects and what advantages it gives our clients.

Built-in SEO

The main advantage of Next.js for business is excellent SEO. Unlike regular React applications, Next.js supports server-side rendering (SSR) and static site generation (SSG).

This means search engines see a fully rendered page rather than empty HTML with JavaScript. The result is fast indexing and high search rankings.

  • Automatic meta tag generation
  • Built-in Open Graph support
  • Automatic sitemap.xml
  • Core Web Vitals optimization

Performance

Next.js optimizes performance at the framework level. Automatic code splitting, image optimization, link prefetching — all of this works out of the box.

For business, this is critical: every second of loading delay can cost conversions. With Next.js, we consistently achieve Lighthouse Scores of 90+.

Developer Experience

Good DX means fast development and fewer bugs. Next.js provides:

  • Hot Module Replacement for instant changes
  • TypeScript out of the box
  • Built-in API Routes
  • Excellent documentation and community

Conclusions

Next.js isn't just a trendy framework — it's a tool that solves real business problems. SEO, performance, scalability — all out of the box.

If you're planning to build a website that needs to bring clients from search and convert visitors — Next.js is the right choice.

conversion-design-principles.title: 7 Principles of Conversion-Driven Design
conversion-design-principles.excerpt: How design impacts sales. Practical techniques that boost conversion.
conversion-design-principles.author: NAKO Agency Team
conversion-design-principles.content:
1. Visual Hierarchy

The user's eye should move across the page in the right direction. Size, color, contrast — these are tools for managing attention.

The main offer should be immediately noticeable. Secondary information — smaller and lighter.

2. Clear CTA

The call-to-action button should be obvious. Contrasting color, clear text, sufficient size.

One main CTA per screen. Don't force the user to choose between multiple equally weighted actions.

3. Whitespace in Design

Empty space is not wasted space. It's a tool that helps highlight what's important and improve readability.

An overloaded design tires users and reduces conversion. Let the content breathe.

4. Trust Elements

Reviews, client logos, certificates, guarantees — everything that confirms your reliability.

Place them near the CTA — this reduces anxiety before making a decision.

5. Loading Speed

Beautiful design is useless if the page takes 10 seconds to load. Optimization is part of design.

Lighthouse Score 90+ is the minimum standard for a commercial website.

seo-for-new-websites.title: SEO for a New Website: Step-by-Step Checklist
seo-for-new-websites.excerpt: What you need to do for SEO before launch and in the first months of your website.
seo-for-new-websites.author: NAKO Agency Team
seo-for-new-websites.content:
Before Launch

SEO starts before the website goes live. Preparation at this stage will save months of work.

  • Keyword research
  • URL structure
  • Content planning
  • Analytics setup

Technical SEO

The foundation without which other efforts are meaningless:

  • HTTPS is mandatory
  • Mobile version
  • Loading speed < 3 sec
  • Proper heading structure
  • XML sitemap
  • robots.txt

Content

Unique, useful content is the foundation of SEO. Every page should answer a specific user query.

Meta title and description for every page. Alt text for images. Internal linking.

First Months

After launch — monitoring and improvements:

  • Search Console registration
  • Position tracking
  • User behavior analysis
  • Regular content additions

expert-website-roi.title: Website ROI for Experts: Real Numbers
expert-website-roi.excerpt: How much a website costs, how many clients it brings, and when it pays for itself. A breakdown with examples.
expert-website-roi.author: NAKO Agency Team
expert-website-roi.content:
Website Cost

A website for an expert costs from €1,000 to €3,000 depending on complexity. It's a one-time investment that works for years.

Traffic Sources

Where clients come from to an expert's website:

  • Organic search (SEO) — 40-60%
  • Social media — 20-30%
  • Referrals — 10-20%
  • Advertising — 10-15%

Conversion

Average conversion for an expert's website is 3-7%. With 1,000 visitors per month, that's 30-70 leads.

Conversion to client is about 30%. Total: 9-21 new clients per month.

ROI Calculation

Example: average consultation fee — €100. 15 clients x €100 = €1,500/month.

With a website cost of €2,000, the payback period is about 1.5 months. After that — pure profit.

tailwind-vs-css-modules.title: Tailwind CSS vs CSS Modules: Which to Choose
tailwind-vs-css-modules.excerpt: Comparing two styling approaches in React projects. Pros, cons, and when to use each.
tailwind-vs-css-modules.author: NAKO Agency Team
tailwind-vs-css-modules.content:
Tailwind Pros

  • Fast development — no need to invent class names
  • Consistency — design tokens out of the box
  • Small final CSS — only used styles
  • Easy theme customization

Tailwind Cons

  • Long class strings in markup
  • Learning curve
  • Complex states require many classes

CSS Modules Pros

  • Familiar CSS syntax
  • Style isolation by default
  • Easy to migrate existing CSS

When to Use Each

Tailwind — for new projects where development speed and consistency matter.

CSS Modules — for projects with an existing design system or a team accustomed to classic CSS.

minimalism-in-web-design.title: Minimalism in Web Design: Trend or Necessity
minimalism-in-web-design.excerpt: Why less is more. How minimalist design affects UX and conversion.
minimalism-in-web-design.author: NAKO Agency Team
minimalism-in-web-design.content:
Why Minimalism Works

Minimalism isn't about emptiness — it's about focus. When there's nothing extra on the page, the user sees what matters.

Research shows: simple pages are perceived as more reliable and professional.

Principles of Minimalism

  • One main focus point per screen
  • Maximum 2-3 colors
  • Plenty of whitespace between elements
  • Clear typography

Common Mistakes

Minimalism does not equal boring. An empty page without focal points isn't minimalism — it's bad design.

Every element should work. If something doesn't carry meaning — remove it.

lighthouse-score-optimization.title: How to Boost Lighthouse Score to 90+
lighthouse-score-optimization.excerpt: A practical guide to website performance optimization. Core Web Vitals, images, code.
lighthouse-score-optimization.author: NAKO Agency Team
lighthouse-score-optimization.content:
Core Web Vitals

Google's three main metrics:

  • LCP (Largest Contentful Paint) < 2.5 sec
  • FID (First Input Delay) < 100 ms
  • CLS (Cumulative Layout Shift) < 0.1

Images

The main source of performance issues:

  • WebP/AVIF formats
  • Lazy loading
  • Proper sizes (srcset)
  • Blur placeholder

Code Optimization

  • Code splitting
  • Tree shaking
  • Minification
  • Removing unused CSS

Fonts

Fonts often block rendering:

  • font-display: swap
  • Preloading critical fonts
  • Character subsetting

landing-page-anatomy.title: Anatomy of a Perfect Landing Page
landing-page-anatomy.excerpt: What blocks a landing page should have and in what order. A structure that sells.
landing-page-anatomy.author: NAKO Agency Team
landing-page-anatomy.content:
Hero Section

The first screen decides everything. In 5 seconds, the user should understand:

  • What you offer
  • Who it's for
  • Why they should stay

Benefits

Not features, but benefits. Not '10 years of experience', but 'We'll solve your problem in 3 days'.

3-5 key benefits with icons or illustrations.

Social Proof

Reviews, case studies, client logos, numbers — everything that backs up your claims.

Place them closer to the CTA — this reduces doubt.

CTA Block

The final call to action. Briefly restate the offer and add a form or button.

Remove everything unnecessary — just the action.
